untranslatable_reason: Signal requires 'Days to expiration' (DTE) and 'Model reliability index' — neither are available in the allowed indicator list. DTE is a temporal market structure variable, not a price/technical indicator. Model reliability index is a custom meta-indicator not derivable from standard OHLCV data. The signal's core logic (reducing model weight, increasing experience-weight) is a portfolio weighting mechanism that cannot be expressed as entry/exit conditions on price action alone.
translation_notes: This is a regime/weighting adjustment rule rather than a directional trading signal. It describes how to reweight decision inputs as expiration approaches (DTE ≤ 5 days) when model confidence drops (reliability < 0.85). The rule is context-dependent and applies to ANY regime. Without access to DTE and model reliability metrics, the rule cannot be operationalized as testable entry/exit conditions on standard technical indicators. A backtest would require custom data feeds for calendar days-to-expiration and a model confidence/reliability score.
